subject: model-loader: reduce model-loader size image
commit_message:
model-loader: reduce model-loader size image

Reducidng the model-loader size image by using
alpine as a base docker image

Change-Id: I61c90a6ed23907008dead0b36b10342bd37d2bda
Issue-ID: INT-798
Signed-off-by: Paul Vaduva <Paul.Vaduva@enea.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-model-loader-master-verify-java/124/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-model-loader-master-verify-java/124

Patch Set 1:

@Harish, @Tian, are you guys OK merging this one?

Patch Set 1:

Hi Paul.  I think this is the first time we've used Alpine as the base image in one of the AAI services.  What type of testing/validation did you do?

I'm 100% on-board with reducing the image size.  As long as you've done some validation to ensure the service starts up and attempts to connect with SDC, I'm happy to give a +2.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Steven. The CSIT jobs are not very easy to run locally so we are basing on each team to guide with the tests as we can't know the logic for all project teams, so if you can give hints on how to run this image and test this I will be happy to put it into practice.
If I simply run the container it complains that:
CONFIG_HOME must be set in order to start up the process

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

Tried in our internal env.  Container fails to start due to nss dependency.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Steven, Can you please expand on this nss dependency? is this a package we can preinstall ? or is something you install at runtime in your environemnt ? I am just trying to undestand if this is a solvable issue or not. For images that are not portable to alpine we abandon the task but we need to undestand if there is a strong reason for not migrating them.

Patch Set 1:

> Hi Steven, Can you please expand on this nss dependency? is this a
 > package we can preinstall ? or is something you install at runtime
 > in your environemnt ? I am just trying to undestand if this is a
 > solvable issue or not. For images that are not portable to alpine
 > we abandon the task but we need to undestand if there is a strong
 > reason for not migrating them.

I don't see any reason why we couldn't pre-install this.  To be honest, I haven't looked at what uses this package.  I imagine that there are a bunch of packages which come with the ubuntu image that aren't by default installed on Alpine.  Will probably be trial and error to determine which ones we'll need to pre-install on alpine.  Which means you'll need to find a way to test the images before you push to gerrit.  I typically use OOM / kubernetes as my testing environment.  There should be instructions on the ONAP wiki under the OOM project for doing this.

Patch Set 1:

I think this is being re-tested elsewhere, do we need to leave this one open?
